label: Programming languages, compilers & type systems

node.label: Programming languages, compilers & type systems
node.definition: Syntax, semantics, intermediate representations, compilation, and types structure executable reasoning and software tools.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Programming languages, compilers & type systems is to connect Discrete mathematics & logic, Algorithms & data structures to downstream capabilities including Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units, Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Graph compilers, runtimes & operator fusion.
examples: substrate for Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units
substrate for Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations
substrate for Graph compilers, runtimes & operator fusion
limitations: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Discrete mathematics & logic, Algorithms & data structures.

dependencies.rationale: Programming languages, compilers & type systems rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Discrete mathematics & logic.
Programming languages, compilers & type systems rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Algorithms & data structures.
